Supercut · creative playbook

coffee

What the longest-running paid social video ads in this market keep doing — counted, with the footage to prove it.

2026-07-12 · 39 ads studied across 38 brands

How we picked these ads

Advertisers kill losing ads fast, so an ad still delivering after a month has earned a look. We searched 200 ads in this market and kept the ones running 30+ days and seen in the last 30. An AI watched each surviving ad and answered a fixed set of questions about it; code then counted the answers. 27 ads that turned out to sell something else were rejected — they are listed at the bottom. No single ad is called a winner here. The findings are patterns that repeat across 39 ads from 38 different brands.

What the studied ads sell

beans & pods10 of 39
brewing equipment7 of 39
drinkware3 of 39
mushroom coffee3 of 39
instant coffee2 of 39
14 one-off segments14 of 39

Top takeaways

34 of 39Show the product in the first 3 seconds to match the longest running ads.
38 of 39Design every video to work without sound by using text overlays.
26 of 39Use a creator style rather than a polished brand look for better reach.
  1. Call out the user's struggle early ugc problem-solution · 11 ads
  2. Lead with a strong visual hook product demo · 10 ads
  3. Make a bold claim for the camera creator testimonial · 9 ads
  4. Use visual hooks and calm vibes lifestyle montage · 5 ads
  5. Create an aspirational brand image still image with text · 3 ads
  6. What to test next
  7. What to double-check

Chapter 1 · ugc problem-solution

Call out the user's struggle early

11 ads 10 brands 222 median days live 3 median cuts in first 10s

This format usually features a person talking directly to the camera. It is the most common way to address specific morning or energy issues.

The first three seconds, side by side

How these ads open

problem callout4 of 11
bold claim3 of 11
visual hook2 of 11
question1 of 11
curiosity tease1 of 11

When the product first appears

first 3 seconds8 of 11
3 to 10 seconds3 of 11

How they ask for the click

hard7 of 11
soft3 of 11
none1 of 11

How to shoot it

  • Open with a specific problem callout. (4 of 11 ads)
  • Show the product within the first 3 seconds. (8 of 11 ads)
  • Perform a full demonstration of the coffee being made. (8 of 11 ads)
  • Keep the energy level high throughout the clip. (7 of 11 ads)
  • Use a direct or hard call to action at the end. (7 of 11 ads)

Examples that lasted

Lynja
Opens with: “Are you tired of your coffee taking too long in the morning?” · 965 days live · 8.6M plays
✨Savanna | ConfidentlyHer✨
Opens with: “Get rid of your milk frother !!!” · 513 days live · 3.0M plays
Creatively Madi
Opens with: “That one girl with the fake Stanley” · 315 days live · 16.2M plays

Watch out

Almost no one in this group uses a slow pace, with only 1 ad doing so.

Chapter 2 · product demo

Lead with a strong visual hook

10 ads 10 brands 171 median days live 3 median cuts in first 10s

These ads focus on the physical coffee and how it is prepared. They work well for equipment and beans.

The first three seconds, side by side

How these ads open

visual hook7 of 10
bold claim1 of 10
social proof1 of 10
problem callout1 of 10

When the product first appears

first 3 seconds10 of 10

How they ask for the click

soft5 of 10
none3 of 10
hard2 of 10

How to shoot it

  • Start with an eye-catching visual of the product. (7 of 10 ads)
  • Ensure the product appears in the first 3 seconds. (10 of 10 ads)
  • Include a full demonstration of the brewing process. (6 of 10 ads)
  • Keep the cuts at a medium pace for clarity. (8 of 10 ads)

Examples that lasted

Proud and Free
239 days live · 13.9M plays
BeanThereBrewedThat
Opens with: “in a holly jolly mood” · 173 days live · 13.2M plays
Alex Dantes-Smith
Opens with: “FELLOW AIDEN Precision Coffee Maker” · 184 days live · 198k plays

Watch out

Only 2 ads in this category use a hard call to action.

Chapter 3 · creator testimonial

Make a bold claim for the camera

9 ads 9 brands 165 median days live 3 median cuts in first 10s

This format relies on a single person's experience and opinion. It feels like a recommendation from a friend.

The first three seconds, side by side

How these ads open

bold claim3 of 9
problem callout2 of 9
social proof1 of 9
curiosity tease1 of 9
question1 of 9
visual hook1 of 9

When the product first appears

first 3 seconds9 of 9

How they ask for the click

soft6 of 9
hard2 of 9
none1 of 9

How to shoot it

  • Start with a bold claim about the coffee. (3 of 9 ads)
  • Show the product immediately in the first 3 seconds. (9 of 9 ads)
  • Show a full demo of how to use or drink the product. (7 of 9 ads)
  • End with a soft invitation rather than a hard sell. (6 of 9 ads)

Examples that lasted

Ryan
Opens with: “would you believe that this latte came from this machine” · 768 days live · 1.9M plays
Kami 🫀
Opens with: “The barista had an attitude so I ordered a VENTI caramel ribbon crunch frappe.” · 545 days live · 12.1M plays
RYZE Superfoods
Opens with: “A must-have coming in hot.” · 161 days live · 21.9M plays

Watch out

Only 1 creator testimonial requires sound to be understood.

Chapter 4 · lifestyle montage

Use visual hooks and calm vibes

5 ads 5 brands 198 median days live 9 median cuts in first 10s

This format relies on many fast cuts to show the coffee fitting into a daily routine. It is less about talking and more about the feeling.

The first three seconds, side by side

How these ads open

visual hook3 of 5
social proof2 of 5

When the product first appears

first 3 seconds4 of 5
3 to 10 seconds1 of 5

How they ask for the click

soft3 of 5
none2 of 5

How to shoot it

  • Prioritize a visual hook to grab attention. (3 of 5 ads)
  • Show the product in the very first 3 seconds. (4 of 5 ads)
  • Maintain a calm emotional tone throughout the cuts. (3 of 5 ads)
  • Use a soft call to action to end the video. (3 of 5 ads)

Examples that lasted

ONYX Coffee Lab
Opens with: “If you're getting a coffee advent calendar this year, get this stunner from Onyx Coffee Lab.” · 214 days live · 449k plays
Elena Joyce
Opens with: “cozy fall afternoons in new york” · 196 days live · 92k plays
L'OR Espresso
176 days live · 207k plays

Watch out

Zero ads in this group use a hard call to action.

Chapter 5 · still image with text

Create an aspirational brand image

3 ads 3 brands 197 median days live 0 median cuts in first 10s
Only 3 ads fit this format — read this chapter as a hint, not a rule.

Simple images with text can run for a long time. They rely on high-quality photography over movement.

The first three seconds, side by side

How these ads open

visual hook1 of 3
offer or discount1 of 3
curiosity tease1 of 3

When the product first appears

first 3 seconds2 of 3
never1 of 3

How they ask for the click

hard2 of 3
soft1 of 3

How to shoot it

  • Focus on a brand-style feel rather than a creator look. (3 of 3 ads)
  • Keep the emotional tone aspirational. (2 of 3 ads)
  • Show the product within the first 3 seconds. (2 of 3 ads)
  • Use a hard call to action text overlay. (2 of 3 ads)

Examples that lasted

The Newsette
Opens with: “TOGETHER IS THE BEST PLACE TO BE” · 194 days live · 211k plays
Aik Cheong Coffee 益昌咖啡
Opens with: “SHOP & WIN” · 204 days live · 4k plays
Alma Coffee
Opens with: “This year, we're leaving Santa something better than milk...” · 197 days live · 10 plays

Watch out

No ads in this small group feature a product demonstration.

What to test next

The Honest Review Hook

Hypothesis
If we open a testimonial with a bold claim, we will see higher engagement.
Why this test
Bold claims are the most popular hook for testimonials, used in 3 out of 9 ads.
Call it on
Hook Rate
Shot notes
  • Record a creator looking directly into the lens
  • Start with a claim about energy or taste
  • Place the bag/cup in the frame from the start
Risk
The claim may feel too much like a scripted ad.
Draft script
This is the only coffee that actually wakes me up. I've tried everything else and nothing compares. Look at this crema. Grab a bag today.

Visual-First Demo

Hypothesis
If we use a visual hook for a product demo, we will increase view time.
Why this test
Visual hooks lead the product demo format, used in 7 out of 10 ads.
Call it on
Average View Duration
Shot notes
  • Focus on close-up shots of steam and pouring
  • Include the full brewing process from start to finish
  • Use a medium pace for the cuts
Risk
Without a spoken hook, viewers may scroll past.
Draft script
(No dialogue) [Visual: Coffee pouring into glass] [Text: Best morning ritual] [Final Shot: Soft CTA button]

The Direct Problem Solver

Hypothesis
If we use a hard CTA with a problem callout, we will drive more clicks.
Why this test
In problem-solution videos, 7 out of 11 ads use a hard CTA.
Call it on
Click-Through Rate
Shot notes
  • Identify a specific morning struggle in the first 2 seconds
  • Keep the energy high and the pace fast
  • End with a clear 'Shop Now' instruction
Risk
High energy can sometimes feel aggressive to calm morning users.
Draft script
Struggling to get out of bed? Stop drinking burnt office coffee and try this. It's a total game changer. Click the link to shop now!

Sample brand brief

This section shows the brief generator on a made-up brand: “Bloom, a small-batch mushroom coffee brand for busy parents”. In the app, you type your own brand and the tests below are rewritten for it.

Bloom is a small-batch mushroom coffee brand tailored for the high-energy demands of busy parents.

Bloom can dominate the market by pairing the 34 ads featuring immediate product visibility with hooks that address parental fatigue. By using energetic creator-led content, which comprises 26 of 39 market ads, Bloom can build trust through authentic morning routines.

The Honest Parent Hook

The idea
A creator-led testimonial featuring a busy parent in a kitchen setting. The ad opens with a direct claim about sustained energy to survive the school run.
The pattern it leans on
Bold claims are the most popular hook for testimonials, used in 3 out of 9 ads, with product visibility in the first 3 seconds for all 9 ads.
Opening line
“This is the only coffee that helps me survive the 6 AM wake-up call without the crash.”
Draft script
[0:00-0:03] Creator holding Bloom mug: This is the only coffee that helps me survive the 6 AM wake-up call without the crash. [0:03-0:10] Shots of Bloom bag and stirring mushroom coffee. [0:10-0:15] Creator taking a sip: No jitters, just focus. Try Bloom for yourself.

Visual Ritual Demo

The idea
A high-quality, brand-led product demo focusing on the sensory experience of brewing small-batch coffee. It uses tight close-ups to show the unique texture of the mushroom blend.
The pattern it leans on
Visual hooks lead the product demo format, used in 7 out of 10 ads, with 10 out of 10 showing the product in the first 3 seconds.
Opening line
“(Visual: Steam rising from a freshly poured cup of Bloom)”
Draft script
[0:00-0:05] Close-up sound of bag opening and powder hitting the cup. [0:05-0:12] Slow pour of hot water and the coffee blooming. [0:12-0:15] Text overlay: The cleaner way to caffeinate. Shop Bloom.

The Fatigue Fixer

The idea
A fast-paced problem-solution video that identifies the morning struggle and offers Bloom as the immediate fix. It utilizes an energetic tone and a direct purchase prompt.
The pattern it leans on
In problem-solution videos, 7 out of 11 ads use a hard CTA, and 4 out of 11 use a specific problem callout hook.
Opening line
“Are you tired of feeling like a zombie until your third cup of coffee?”
Draft script
[0:00-0:03] Split screen: Exhausted parent vs. Parent drinking Bloom. Hook: Are you tired of feeling like a zombie until your third cup of coffee? [0:03-0:10] Quick cuts of the small-batch brewing process. [0:10-0:15] High energy: Get the focus you need. Click shop now!

What to double-check

Everything above comes from an AI watching ads one at a time and code counting its answers. Here is where that process was noisy, and what it threw out.

Ads rejected as off-market (27)

The search also returned ads selling something else. They were dropped before any counting, so they do not touch the numbers above.

AdvertiserWhy it was dropped
livoff-market: sells "This ad sells Poppy, a prebiotic soda, by demonstrating how to use it to create a Shirley Temple float with a Starbucks Frappuccino."
Expand Furnitureoff-market: sells "Space-saving furniture, specifically a coffee table that expands into a dining table and a matching extendable bench."
𝐌 𝐔 𝐋 𝐀 𝐍 🪭off-market: sells "This ad sells a wooden coffee table with a lift-top storage compartment."
The Wooblesoff-market: sells "Crochet kits for creating plush characters and objects."
Maria’s Munchiesoff-market: sells "A coffee recipe app or platform providing instructions for making Starbucks-style drinks at home."
Nanaoff-market: sells "A lift-top coffee table with storage space."
lamarzoccohomeukoff-market: sells "Tickets or registration for 'Out of The Box 2025', a coffee-themed event/festival featuring live demos and community conversations."
BEASTRO Dubaioff-market: sells "This ad sells dining services and food items at Beastro, a restaurant located in First Central Hotel Suites."

Ads we could not analyze (4)

BrüMatevideo download failed (curl exit 22)

BrüMatevideo download failed (curl exit 22)
BrüMatevideo download failed (curl exit 22)

How reliable was the watching?

We measured the editing pace of every ad twice: once by the AI's eye, once by scene-detection software. The AI was off by 2.8 cuts on average, and within 2 cuts of the software on 51% of ads. The cut counts printed in the chapters are the software's, not the AI's.

Where a spoken hook could be checked against the ad library's own transcript (9 ads), the AI's quote matched 9 times and disagreed 0 times. The other 30 ads had on-screen-text hooks, no hook, or no transcript — nothing to check against.

The AI reported firm format labels for every ad in the pool.

Assumptions this report makes

  • "Still delivering" means the ad was seen in the ad library within the last 30 days. The data offers no direct "currently live" flag.
  • Days-running and play counts come from the ad library and are taken at face value.
  • A long-running ad earned study, but nothing here proves any single ad was profitable. Only the repeated patterns are conclusions.
  • Spoken quotes were transcribed by the AI from the audio; where the ad library had its own transcript, we cross-checked (see above).

Study ranking: ads are ordered for the example lists by a score of longevity ×0.5 + engagement ×0.3 + formatConfidence ×0.2, each part normalized within this pool, with at most one ad per brand in any list.

Made with Supercut. Days-running and play counts come from the ad library data; "still delivering" means seen in the last 30 days, the closest signal the data offers to "currently live".
